description: A somewhat large empid, with very greenish-olive upperparts. Crown also greenish-olive but slighter darker than the back. Dull white eye-ring somewhat broader posteriorly but not tear-shaped. Lores a dull white. Dark eye. Sides of face and nape grayer than the back. White chin and throat very much contrasting with the face. Whitish upperbreast. Distinguishable breast band about the same color as the back but faded in the center. Belly had a yellowish wash. The rest of the underparts yellowish white. The bill appeared wide and somewhat long and was completely yellow-orange on the lower mandible and blackish on the upper mandible. Fairly long wings, grayish-black in color with two bold obvious contrasting white wing-bars. Well-defined tertial edges with white on outer web, secondaries also edged with white, and primaries tipped with narrow white. Long primary extension. Broad, medium length tail. Upper tail similar in color to wings but not quite as dark. Legs and feet blackish.Behaviors: This bird was actively flycatching in the woods at Dixon. It repeatedly returned to a medium to low exposed perch, but not always to the same tree. While perched the bird was generally quiet with little movement other than looking around.Call: not heardPlumage: adult
